steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Some experimental and validated works are available from the literature. Refs. [[12], [13], [14]] have been chosen and deeply investigated in order to validate the current work. The detailed information of the validation conditions is as follows:

steps.title: Receiver optimization methodology
steps.instruction: The characteristics of the ANSYS Design Xplorer tool are used in this work. The optimization method proposed is the combination of the Kriging surface response method and MOGA. It enables optimization studies based on multiple objectives to take account of design uncertainties and to determine the best way to improve the reliability of the product. The framework generates a set of parameters (using Design of Experiments DOE) based on a mathematical sampling method. Output parameters are generated based on the parameter configured to construct response surfaces. Finally, using an optimization algorithm, optimal results are obtained on the generated response surfaces. The Response Surface (RSM) allows approximations via support points to represent the relationship between the output parameter and the input parameters (design parameters) based on regression methods [18,20]. Compared to the classic MOGA algorithm, the use of the Kriging meta-model allows a faster optimization process [20].
